name: Noble gases — group number
question_text: Which group of the modern periodic table contains the noble gases (helium, neon, argon, etc.)?
general_feedback: Noble gases occupy group 18, the right-most column of the periodic table. Their full outer electron shells make them chemically unreactive under most conditions.
correct_feedback: Correct — Group 18 (the final column) houses He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, and Rn.

answers.feedback: Group 18 (the final column) houses He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, and Rn.
Group 1 contains the alkali metals — very reactive, the opposite of noble gases.
Group 17 is the halogens; noble gases are one column further right.
Group 14 contains C, Si, Ge, Sn, Pb — not noble gases.
